Augustus, the first Roman emperor, holds the record as the longest-reigning Roman emperor, ruling for 40 years from 27 BCE to 14 CE.[1](https://study.com/academy/lesson/timeline-of-roman-emperors.html), [2](https://jaypenner.com/blog/roman-emperors-and-their-rule), [3](https://online.norwich.edu/online/about/resource-library/7-major-emperors-during-rise-roman-empire), [4](https://www.thecollector.com/augustus-rome-longest-reigning-emperor/), [5](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qVHKYt_tj-4) He was succeeded by his stepson and adopted son, Tiberius.[6](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Julio-Claudian-dynasty), [7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Augustus), [8](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Tiberius/Reign-as-emperor), [9](https://www.thecollector.com/heirs-augustus-lucius-gaius-caesar-roman-dioscuri/)

Augustus's reign ushered in an era of peace known as the Pax Romana, and he established the Julio-Claudian dynasty.[1](https://study.com/academy/lesson/timeline-of-roman-emperors.html), [6](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Julio-Claudian-dynasty), [3](https://online.norwich.edu/online/about/resource-library/7-major-emperors-during-rise-roman-empire) Despite having several other potential heirs, including his grandsons Gaius and Lucius Caesar who both died young, Augustus ultimately chose Tiberius as his successor.[8](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Tiberius/Reign-as-emperor), [10](https://www.reddit.com/r/ancientrome/comments/1odflhh/the_succession_crisis_of_augustus/), [9](https://www.thecollector.com/heirs-augustus-lucius-gaius-caesar-roman-dioscuri/) Tiberius, a member of the aristocratic Claudia gens, became emperor in 14 CE and reigned until 37 CE.[6](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Julio-Claudian-dynasty), [8](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Tiberius/Reign-as-emperor)
